readme:
# RAG on Oracle AI Database 26ai

A complete Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) application that runs entirely on Oracle AI Database 26ai. Embeddings are generated inside the database, vectors live alongside the source text, and LangChain orchestrates the chat. No external embedding service. No separate vector store. No paid APIs in the critical path.

The demo answers questions about Oracle AI Database 26ai by retrieving relevant passages from three of Oracle's official documentation PDFs and grounding the LLM's response in the source text. Each answer ships with citations back to the exact pages used.

## Highlights

- **In-database embeddings.** The pre-built `all-MiniLM-L12-v2` ONNX model lives inside Oracle. Embeddings are generated by SQL with `VECTOR_EMBEDDING()`, no external service needed.
- **Native vector storage.** Each chunk is one row with a `VECTOR(384, FLOAT32)` column. HNSW index for fast similarity search.
- **LangChain integration.** The official `langchain-oracledb` package powers retrieval. About 20 lines of LangChain Expression Language (LCEL).
- **Multi-document corpus.** Pulls from three Oracle docs (Vector Search, JSON, Database Concepts) so cross-document retrieval is visible.
- **Free to run.** Oracle AI Database Free in Docker, local LLM through Ollama. Zero external API spend.

## What you'll need

| Tool | Why | Install |
|---|---|---|
| Docker Desktop or Podman | Runs Oracle AI Database Free locally | [docker.com](https://www.docker.com) |
| Python 3.11 or higher | Runs the seed script and the chat UI | [python.org](https://www.python.org) |
| Ollama | Runs a local LLM so no paid API is required | [ollama.com](https://ollama.com) |

About 15 GB of free disk for the Oracle image and the local LLM.

## Setup

These steps take you from a fresh clone to a working chat in about 10 minutes (most of which is the database starting up and the embedding model downloading).

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Emminex23/rag-on-oracle-26ai.git
cd rag-on-oracle-26ai
```

### 2. Run the one-time setup

```bash
make setup
```

This creates a Python virtual environment in `.venv/`, installs every package in `requirements.txt`, and copies `.env.example` to `.env`.

### 3. Start the database

```bash
make db-up
```

The first time you run this, Docker pulls the Oracle Free image (about 10 GB). Subsequent starts take seconds.

The database needs about 60 seconds to initialize on cold start. Check progress:

```bash
make db-status
```

When STATUS shows `(healthy)`, you're ready.

### 4. Configure the database for HNSW indexing

```bash
make db-init
```

This connects to the database as SYSDBA, sets `VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE = 512M`, and restarts the database. The 512 MB allocation enables in-memory HNSW vector indexes.

The restart takes about 60 seconds. Wait for `make db-status` to show `(healthy)` again.

### 5. Pull the local LLM

```bash
ollama pull llama3.2:3b
```

This downloads about 2 GB. Run it once and forget about it.

### 6. Load the documents and embeddings

```bash
make seed
```

This script:

1. Downloads the pre-built `all-MiniLM-L12-v2` ONNX embedding model from Oracle (about 130 MB, one time).
2. Downloads three Oracle documentation PDFs (about 25 MB total, one time).
3. Creates the demo user (`raguser`) with the right tablespace and privileges.
4. Loads the ONNX model into the database so embeddings can be generated in SQL.
5. Chunks every PDF and inserts each chunk into the `DOC_CHUNKS` table. Embeddings are generated by the database itself with `VECTOR_EMBEDDING()`.
6. Builds an HNSW vector index for fast similarity search.

The whole script runs in about 90 seconds.

### 7. Launch the chat UI

```bash
make run
```

Streamlit will open `http://localhost:8501` in your browser. Try the suggested questions or ask your own.

## Architecture

A typical RAG application stitches together at least three different services: an embedding API, a dedicated vector database, and a separate store for the original text and metadata. Each service costs money, adds latency, and is one more thing that can break.

![Typical Multi-Service RAG Stack](docs/images/multi_service_rag.svg)

This project collapses all of that into a single Oracle AI Database. The embedding model, the vectors, the original text, and the metadata all live inside the database. The only piece that runs outside is the LLM, and even that runs locally through Ollama, so the whole application makes zero external API calls.

![RAG on Oracle AI Database 26ai](docs/images/oracle_only_rag.svg)

The full request flow looks like this:

![RAG on Oracle AI Database 26ai architecture](docs/images/architecture.svg)

## Try these questions

The corpus spans three documents, so the most interesting questions are ones that pull from across them. Each answer panel shows which document each retrieved chunk came from.

**From a single document:**

- *How do I load an ONNX embedding model into Oracle?* (Vector Search Guide)
- *What is JSON Relational Duality?* (JSON Developer's Guide)
- *How does ACID consistency work in Oracle?* (Database Concepts)

**Cross-document (the interesting ones):**

- *Can I query JSON columns with vector search?*
- *How does ACID consistency apply to vector data?*
- *Compare HNSW indexes with regular B-tree indexes.*

## Project structure

```
rag-on-oracle-26ai/
├── README.md             this file
├── docker-compose.yml    starts Oracle AI Database 26ai locally
├── requirements.txt      Python dependencies
├── .env.example          environment variable template
├── Makefile              one-command shortcuts
├── seed.py               loads the ONNX model, the PDFs, and the chunks
├── app.py                Streamlit chat UI with LangChain LCEL chain
├── retrieval.sql         standalone retrieval query
├── data/                 model and PDF downloads (gitignored)
├── docs/
│   └── images/
│       ├── architecture.svg
│       ├── multi_service_rag.svg
│       └── oracle_only_rag.svg
└── scripts/
    └── init_db.sql       one-time database configuration
```

## Make targets

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `make setup` | Create venv, install dependencies, copy `.env` |
| `make db-up` | Start the Oracle Database container |
| `make db-status` | Show database container status |
| `make db-init` | Configure VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE for HNSW (one-time) |
| `make db-down` | Stop the database (keeps data) |
| `make seed` | Load the embedding model and source PDFs |
| `make run` | Launch the Streamlit chat UI |
| `make clean` | Remove downloaded data files (keeps DB and venv) |
| `make reset` | Wipe everything and start over |

| Command | What it does |

| `make clean` | Remove downloaded data files (keeps DB and venv) |
| `make reset` | Wipe everything and start over |

docker-compose.yml.content:
services:
  oracle:
    image: container-registry.oracle.com/database/free:latest
    container_name: oracle-26ai-rag
    ports:
      - "1521:1521"
    environment:
      ORACLE_PWD: Welcome12345
      ORACLE_CHARACTERSET: AL32UTF8
    volumes:
      - oracle-data:/opt/oracle/oradata
      - ./data:/opt/oracle/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "/opt/oracle/checkDBStatus.sh"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 20
      start_period: 60s

volumes:
  oracle-data:

docs/images/architecture.svg.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 800 760" font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif">

  <!-- Title -->
  <text x="400" y="40" font-size="22" font-weight="700" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">
    RAG on Oracle AI Database 26ai
  </text>

  <!-- User question (input) -->
  <rect x="50" y="280" width="160" height="60" rx="8" fill="#a5d8ff" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="130" y="316" font-size="16" font-weight="600" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">User question</text>

  <!-- Arrow: question -> database -->
  <line x1="210" y1="310" x2="270" y2="310" stroke="#1e1e1e" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

  <!-- Oracle AI Database 26ai container -->
  <rect x="280" y="120" width="380" height="380" rx="10" fill="#fff3bf" fill-opacity="0.4" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="300" y="155" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#9a5030">Oracle AI Database 26ai</text>

  <!-- DOC_CHUNKS table -->
  <rect x="310" y="185" width="320" height="55" rx="8" fill="#c3fae8" stroke="#0d9488" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="470" y="218" font-size="15" font-weight="600" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">DOC_CHUNKS table</text>

  <!-- VECTOR column -->
  <rect x="310" y="250" width="320" height="55" rx="8" fill="#c3fae8" stroke="#0d9488" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="470" y="283" font-size="15" font-weight="600" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">VECTOR(384, FLOAT32)</text>

  <!-- HNSW index -->
  <rect x="310" y="315" width="320" height="55" rx="8" fill="#c3fae8" stroke="#0d9488" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="470" y="348" font-size="15" font-weight="600" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">HNSW index</text>

  <!-- ONNX embedding model -->
  <rect x="310" y="380" width="320" height="70" rx="8" fill="#d0bfff" stroke="#7c3aed" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="470" y="410" font-size="15" font-weight="600" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">ONNX embedding model</text>
  <text x="470" y="432" font-size="14" fill="#475569" text-anchor="middle">(in-database)</text>

  <!-- Arrow: database -> top-5 chunks -->
  <line x1="470" y1="500" x2="470" y2="540" stroke="#1e1e1e" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>
  <text x="490" y="525" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#475569">top-5 chunks</text>

  <!-- Local LLM (Ollama) -->
  <rect x="320" y="555" width="300" height="80" rx="10" fill="#ffd8a8" stroke="#c2410c" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="470" y="590" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">Local LLM (Ollama)</text>
  <text x="470" y="613" font-size="15" font-weight="500" fill="#475569" text-anchor="middle">llama3.2:3b</text>

  <!-- Arrow: LLM -> answer -->
  <line x1="470" y1="635" x2="470" y2="675" stroke="#1e1e1e" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

  <!-- Grounded answer + citations -->
  <rect x="270" y="690" width="400" height="60" rx="8" fill="#b2f2bb" stroke="#15803d" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="470" y="726" font-size="17" font-weight="700" fill="#1e1e1e" text-anchor="middle">Grounded answer + citations</text>

  <!-- Arrow marker definition -->
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z" fill="#1e1e1e"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

</svg>

docs/images/multi_service_rag.svg.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 1200 600" font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif">

  <!-- Dark background -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="1200" height="600" fill="#1e1e2e"/>

  <!-- Title -->
  <text x="600" y="60" font-size="32" font-weight="700" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">
    Typical Multi-Service RAG Stack
  </text>

  <!-- Documents -->
  <rect x="60" y="220" width="180" height="100" rx="10" fill="#1e3a5f" stroke="#4a9eed" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="150" y="278" font-size="22" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Documents</text>

  <!-- Arrow 1 -->
  <line x1="240" y1="270" x2="320" y2="270" stroke="#a0a0a0" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

  <!-- OpenAI Embeddings -->
  <rect x="320" y="220" width="200" height="100" rx="10" fill="#5c1a1a" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="420" y="270" font-size="20" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">OpenAI Embeddings</text>
  <text x="420" y="298" font-size="15" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">costs $ per call</text>

  <!-- Arrow 2 -->
  <line x1="520" y1="270" x2="600" y2="270" stroke="#a0a0a0" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

  <!-- Pinecone -->
  <rect x="600" y="220" width="200" height="100" rx="10" fill="#5c1a1a" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="700" y="270" font-size="22" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Pinecone</text>
  <text x="700" y="298" font-size="15" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">vector database, $</text>

  <!-- Postgres (below Pinecone) -->
  <rect x="600" y="380" width="200" height="100" rx="10" fill="#5c3d1a" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="700" y="430" font-size="22" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Postgres</text>
  <text x="700" y="458" font-size="15" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle">text and metadata</text>

  <!-- Sync arrow between Pinecone and Postgres (bidirectional, dashed) -->
  <line x1="700" y1="320" x2="700" y2="380" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="6,4" marker-end="url(#arrowOrange)" marker-start="url(#arrowOrangeStart)"/>
  <text x="722" y="358" font-size="14" fill="#f59e0b">sync</text>

  <!-- Arrow 3 (Pinecone to LLM) -->
  <line x1="800" y1="270" x2="880" y2="270" stroke="#a0a0a0" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

  <!-- LLM -->
  <rect x="880" y="220" width="180" height="100" rx="10" fill="#1e3a5f" stroke="#4a9eed" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="970" y="278" font-size="22" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">LLM</text>

  <!-- Bottom caption -->
  <text x="600" y="555" font-size="20" fill="#a0a0a0" text-anchor="middle">
    3 services. 3 sets of credentials. 3 things that can break.
  </text>

  <!-- Arrow markers -->
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z" fill="#a0a0a0"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arrowOrange" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z" fill="#f59e0b"/>
    </marker>
    <marker id="arrowOrangeStart" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="1" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto">
      <path d="M 10 0 L 0 5 L 10 10 z" fill="#f59e0b"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

</svg>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 1200 600" font-family="-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif">

  <!-- Dark background -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="1200" height="600" fill="#1e1e2e"/>

  <!-- Title -->
  <text x="600" y="60" font-size="32" font-weight="700" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">
    RAG on Oracle AI Database 26ai
  </text>

  <!-- Documents -->

  <text x="120" y="298" font-size="22" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Documents</text>

  <!-- Arrow into Oracle box -->
  <line x1="200" y1="290" x2="280" y2="290" stroke="#a0a0a0" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

  <!-- Oracle container box (the main story) -->
  <rect x="280" y="130" width="540" height="320" rx="14" fill="#1a4d2e" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="3"/>
  <text x="550" y="170" font-size="24" font-weight="700" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Oracle AI Database 26ai</text>

  <!-- Nested: ONNX Model -->
  <rect x="310" y="200" width="220" height="70" rx="8" fill="#1e3a5f" stroke="#4a9eed" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="420" y="242" font-size="17" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">ONNX Embedding Model</text>

  <!-- Nested: Vectors -->
  <rect x="570" y="200" width="220" height="70" rx="8" fill="#2d1b69" stroke="#8b5cf6" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="680" y="242" font-size="17" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Vectors (HNSW Index)</text>

  <!-- Nested: Original Text -->
  <rect x="310" y="290" width="220" height="70" rx="8" fill="#1a4d4d" stroke="#06b6d4" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="420" y="332" font-size="17" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Original Text</text>

  <!-- Nested: Metadata -->
  <rect x="570" y="290" width="220" height="70" rx="8" fill="#1a4d4d" stroke="#06b6d4" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="680" y="332" font-size="17" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Metadata</text>

  <!-- Inner caption -->
  <text x="550" y="408" font-size="15" font-style="italic" fill="#a0a0a0" text-anchor="middle">
    one query, no external services
  </text>

  <!-- Arrow out of Oracle box -->
  <line x1="820" y1="290" x2="900" y2="290" stroke="#a0a0a0" stroke-width="2.5" marker-end="url(#arrow)"/>

  <!-- Local LLM -->
  <rect x="900" y="240" width="200" height="100" rx="10" fill="#1e3a5f" stroke="#4a9eed" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="1000" y="285" font-size="20" font-weight="600" fill="#e5e5e5" text-anchor="middle">Local LLM</text>
  <text x="1000" y="310" font-size="16" fill="#a0a0a0" text-anchor="middle">(Ollama)</text>

  <!-- Bottom caption -->
  <text x="600" y="510" font-size="20" fill="#a0a0a0" text-anchor="middle">
    1 database. 0 external API calls. Everything in one place.
  </text>

  <!-- Arrow markers -->
  <defs>
    <marker id="arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="9" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse">
      <path d="M 0 0 L 10 5 L 0 10 z" fill="#a0a0a0"/>
    </marker>
  </defs>

</svg>

requirements.txt.content:
oracledb>=2.5.0
langchain>=1.0.0

retrieval.sql.content:
-- retrieval.sql
--
-- The complete retrieval layer for the RAG demo. Eight lines of SQL.
-- Runs entirely inside Oracle AI Database 26ai. No external embedding
-- service. No separate vector store. No application-side merging.
--
-- 1. VECTOR_EMBEDDING(...) calls the all-MiniLM-L12-v2 ONNX model that
--    was loaded into the database with DBMS_VECTOR.LOAD_ONNX_MODEL.
--    It returns a 384-dimension FLOAT32 vector for the user's question.
--
-- 2. VECTOR_DISTANCE(...) compares that question vector against every
--    chunk vector using cosine similarity. The HNSW vector index makes
--    this fast.
--
-- 3. FETCH APPROX FIRST 5 ROWS ONLY tells the optimizer to use the
--    HNSW index for an Approximate Nearest Neighbor scan.
--
-- Bind variable :question is the user's question text.

SELECT
    chunk_text,
    source_page,
    VECTOR_DISTANCE(
        embedding,
        VECTOR_EMBEDDING(ALL_MINILM_L12_V2 USING :question AS data),
        COSINE
    ) AS distance
FROM DOC_CHUNKS
ORDER BY distance
FETCH APPROX FIRST 5 ROWS ONLY;

scripts/init_db.sql.content:
-- ============================================================================
-- init_db.sql
-- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
-- One-time database configuration for the RAG demo.
--
-- Allocates 512 MB of vector memory to enable in-memory HNSW (Hierarchical
-- Navigable Small World) vector indexes. Without this, the seed script falls
-- back to IVF (Inverted File Flat) indexes, which work fine but trade memory
-- residency for slightly slower searches at scale.
--
-- Required: must be run as SYSDBA. The Makefile target `make db-init` does
-- this automatically. To run manually:
--
--     docker exec -it oracle-26ai-rag sqlplus / as sysdba
--     SQL> @/path/to/init_db.sql
--
-- The database will shut down and restart, which takes about 60 seconds.
-- ============================================================================

ALTER SYSTEM SET VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE = 512M SCOPE = SPFILE;

SHUTDOWN IMMEDIATE
STARTUP

-- Verify the setting took effect.
SHOW PARAMETER vector_memory_size;

EXIT;

seed.py.content:
"""seed.py - load Oracle docs into Oracle AI Database 26ai with in-DB embeddings.

Two-phase setup:
  Phase 1 (as SYSTEM): create a dedicated demo user with the USERS tablespace,
  create the ONNX directory, grant privileges. Required because VECTOR columns
  cannot live in the SYSTEM tablespace (ORA-43853).

  Phase 2 (as the demo user): load the ONNX embedding model, create the chunks
  table, insert the chunks (embeddings generated by VECTOR_EMBEDDING inside
  the database), and build the HNSW index.
"""

import json
import os
import sys
from pathlib import Path

import oracledb
import requests
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from langchain_community.document_loaders import PyPDFLoader
from langchain_text_splitters import RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter

load_dotenv()

# Admin connection (used only for one-time setup of user + directory).
ADMIN_USER = os.getenv("ORACLE_ADMIN_USER", "system")
ADMIN_PASSWORD = os.getenv("ORACLE_ADMIN_PASSWORD", "Welcome12345")

# Demo application user. All RAG data lives in this user's schema.
APP_USER = os.getenv("ORACLE_USER", "raguser")
APP_PASSWORD = os.getenv("ORACLE_PASSWORD", "RagUser_2026")
ORACLE_DSN = os.getenv("ORACLE_DSN", "localhost:1521/FREEPDB1")

DATA_DIR = Path(os.getenv("DATA_DIR", "./data"))
DB_ONNX_DIR = os.getenv("DB_ONNX_DIR", "/opt/oracle/data")

ONNX_MODEL_NAME = "ALL_MINILM_L12_V2"
ONNX_FILE_NAME = "all_MiniLM_L12_v2.onnx"
ONNX_URL = os.getenv(
    "ONNX_URL",
    "https://adwc4pm.objectstorage.us-ashburn-1.oci.customer-oci.com/p/"
    "iPX9W0MZeRkwJKWdFmdJCemmN-iKAl_bFvNGYLW7YqIrw4kKsukL24J2q93Beb9S/n/"
    "adwc4pm/b/OML-ai-models/o/all_MiniLM_L12_v2.onnx",
)
PDF_FILE_NAME = "ai-vector-search-users-guide.pdf"  # legacy, kept for reference

# Documents to ingest into the RAG corpus. Each entry has:
#   - url: the public download URL
#   - filename: where it gets saved locally inside DATA_DIR
#   - source_name: a human-readable label that will be tagged on every chunk
#                  from this document so the UI can show "from: <source_name>"
DOCUMENTS = [
    {
        "url": (
            "https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/23/"
            "vecse/ai-vector-search-users-guide.pdf"
        ),
        "filename": "ai-vector-search-users-guide.pdf",
        "source_name": "AI Vector Search User's Guide",
    },
    {
        "url": (
            "https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/23/"
            "adjsn/json-developers-guide.pdf"
        ),
        "filename": "json-developers-guide.pdf",
        "source_name": "JSON Developer's Guide",
    },
    {
        "url": (
            "https://docs.oracle.com/en/database/oracle/oracle-database/23/"
            "cncpt/database-concepts.pdf"
        ),
        "filename": "database-concepts.pdf",
        "source_name": "Database Concepts",
    },
]

CHUNK_SIZE = 2000
CHUNK_OVERLAP = 200

DATA_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)


def download_if_missing(url, dest):
    """Download a file with progress, using requests (which uses certifi)."""
    if dest.exists() and dest.stat().st_size > 0:
        size_mb = dest.stat().st_size / (1024 * 1024)
        print(f"  -> {dest.name} already present ({size_mb:.1f} MB), skipping")
        return
    print(f"  -> downloading {dest.name}")
    response = requests.get(url, stream=True, timeout=60)
    response.raise_for_status()
    total = int(response.headers.get("content-length", 0))
    downloaded = 0
    with open(dest, "wb") as fh:
        for chunk in response.iter_content(chunk_size=1024 * 1024):
            if not chunk:
                continue
            fh.write(chunk)
            downloaded += len(chunk)
            if total:
                pct = (downloaded / total) * 100
                print(
                    f"     {downloaded / (1024 * 1024):6.1f} MB / "
                    f"{total / (1024 * 1024):6.1f} MB  ({pct:5.1f}%)",
                    end="\r",
                )
    if total:
        print()
    print(f"  -> saved to {dest}")


# Oracle error codes that mean "the thing you're dropping doesn't exist".
# Different Oracle packages raise different codes for the same condition,
# so we suppress all of them in safe_execute.
_NONEXISTENT_ERROR_CODES = (
    942,    # ORA-00942: table does not exist
    1418,   # ORA-01418: index does not exist
    1918,   # ORA-01918: user does not exist
    4043,   # ORA-04043: object does not exist
    40284,  # ORA-40284: model does not exist (DBMS_VECTOR variant)
    40287,  # ORA-40287: model does not exist (DBMS_DATA_MINING variant)
)


def safe_execute(cursor, sql):
    """Run SQL, suppressing 'does not exist' errors so the script is rerunnable."""
    try:
        cursor.execute(sql)
    except oracledb.DatabaseError as exc:
        (error,) = exc.args
        if error.code not in _NONEXISTENT_ERROR_CODES:
            raise


def setup_user_and_directory(admin_conn):
    """Phase 1: as SYSTEM, drop and recreate the demo user with the USERS
    tablespace, then create the ONNX directory and grant access on it."""
    cursor = admin_conn.cursor()

    # Force-disconnect any existing sessions for the demo user so DROP USER
    # can succeed cleanly. Ignore errors if nothing is connected.
    try:
        cursor.execute(
            f"""
            BEGIN
                FOR s IN (
                    SELECT sid, serial# FROM v$session
                    WHERE username = UPPER('{APP_USER}')
                ) LOOP
                    EXECUTE IMMEDIATE
                        'ALTER SYSTEM KILL SESSION ''' ||
                        s.sid || ',' || s.serial# || ''' IMMEDIATE';
                END LOOP;
            END;
            """
        )
    except oracledb.DatabaseError:
        pass

    # Drop and recreate the user. Drop is suppressed if the user doesn't exist.
    safe_execute(cursor, f"DROP USER {APP_USER} CASCADE")
    cursor.execute(
        f"CREATE USER {APP_USER} IDENTIFIED BY {APP_PASSWORD} "
        f"DEFAULT TABLESPACE USERS QUOTA UNLIMITED ON USERS"
    )

    # Privileges:
    #   CONNECT, RESOURCE: basic schema usage
    #   DB_DEVELOPER_ROLE: bundles common developer privileges (CREATE TABLE, etc.)
    #   CREATE MINING MODEL: required to load the ONNX embedding model
    cursor.execute(
        f"GRANT CONNECT, RESOURCE, DB_DEVELOPER_ROLE, "
        f"CREATE MINING MODEL TO {APP_USER}"
    )

    # Create the directory object that maps to the host path where the ONNX
    # file lives. The Oracle container has /opt/oracle/data mounted from ./data.
    cursor.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE DIRECTORY ONNX_DIR AS '{DB_ONNX_DIR}'")
    cursor.execute(f"GRANT READ, WRITE ON DIRECTORY ONNX_DIR TO {APP_USER}")

    admin_conn.commit()
    cursor.close()


def main():
    print(f"Step 1: download embedding model and {len(DOCUMENTS)} document(s)")
    download_if_missing(ONNX_URL, DATA_DIR / ONNX_FILE_NAME)
    for doc in DOCUMENTS:
        download_if_missing(doc["url"], DATA_DIR / doc["filename"])

    print(f"\nStep 2: connect as {ADMIN_USER} to set up the demo user")
    admin_conn = oracledb.connect(
        user=ADMIN_USER, password=ADMIN_PASSWORD, dsn=ORACLE_DSN
    )
    print(f"  -> connected as {ADMIN_USER}")
    setup_user_and_directory(admin_conn)
    print(f"  -> user {APP_USER} ready (USERS tablespace, all needed grants)")
    print(f"  -> directory ONNX_DIR points at {DB_ONNX_DIR}")
    admin_conn.close()

    print(f"\nStep 3: reconnect as {APP_USER} for the rest of the seed")
    connection = oracledb.connect(
        user=APP_USER, password=APP_PASSWORD, dsn=ORACLE_DSN
    )
    cursor = connection.cursor()
    print(f"  -> connected as {APP_USER}")

    print("\nStep 4: load the ONNX model into the database")
    safe_execute(
        cursor,
        f"BEGIN DBMS_VECTOR.DROP_ONNX_MODEL("
        f"model_name => '{ONNX_MODEL_NAME}', force => TRUE); END;",
    )
    cursor.execute(
        f"""
        BEGIN
            DBMS_VECTOR.LOAD_ONNX_MODEL(
                directory  => 'ONNX_DIR',
                file_name  => '{ONNX_FILE_NAME}',
                model_name => '{ONNX_MODEL_NAME}'
            );
        END;
        """
    )
    print(f"  -> model {ONNX_MODEL_NAME} loaded")

    print("\nStep 5: create DOC_CHUNKS table")
    safe_execute(cursor, "DROP INDEX DOC_CHUNKS_VEC_IDX")
    safe_execute(cursor, "DROP INDEX DOC_CHUNKS_HNSW_IDX")  # legacy from earlier runs
    safe_execute(cursor, "DROP TABLE DOC_CHUNKS")
    # Schema matches LangChain OracleVS conventions: ID, TEXT, METADATA, EMBEDDING.
    # METADATA uses Oracle 23ai+ native JSON type so python-oracledb auto-
    # deserializes it into a Python dict on retrieval. (CLOB would come back as
    # a raw string and break OracleVS's metadata parsing.)
    cursor.execute(
        """
        CREATE TABLE DOC_CHUNKS (
            ID         VARCHAR2(64) DEFAULT SYS_GUID() PRIMARY KEY,
            TEXT       CLOB,
            METADATA   JSON,
            EMBEDDING  VECTOR(384, FLOAT32)
        )
        """
    )
    print("  -> table DOC_CHUNKS ready in USERS tablespace")

    print(f"\nStep 6: read and chunk {len(DOCUMENTS)} document(s)")
    splitter = RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter(
        chunk_size=CHUNK_SIZE, chunk_overlap=CHUNK_OVERLAP
    )
    chunks = []
    for doc in DOCUMENTS:
        loader = PyPDFLoader(str(DATA_DIR / doc["filename"]))
        pages = loader.load()
        doc_chunks = splitter.split_documents(pages)
        # Tag every chunk from this document with its source name so the UI
        # can show "from: <source_name>" and so we can filter by source if needed.
        for chunk in doc_chunks:
            chunk.metadata["source_name"] = doc["source_name"]
        chunks.extend(doc_chunks)
        print(f"  -> {doc['source_name']}: {len(doc_chunks)} chunks")
    print(f"  -> total: {len(chunks)} chunks across {len(DOCUMENTS)} document(s)")

    print("\nStep 7: insert chunks (embeddings generated in the database)")
    insert_sql = """
        INSERT INTO DOC_CHUNKS (TEXT, METADATA, EMBEDDING)
        VALUES (
            :text,
            JSON(:metadata),
            VECTOR_EMBEDDING(ALL_MINILM_L12_V2 USING :text AS data)
        )
    """

    # Build the full list of bind parameters once, then use executemany() to
    # send everything in a single round trip. This is roughly 5-10x faster than
    # cursor.execute() in a loop because we avoid the per-row network latency.
    rows = [
        {
            "text": chunk.page_content,
            "metadata": json.dumps(
                {
                    "source_name": chunk.metadata.get("source_name", "unknown"),
                    "source_page": int(chunk.metadata.get("page", 0)),
                }
            ),
        }
        for chunk in chunks
    ]

    # Insert in batches of 200 so the progress indicator updates a few times
    # for very large document sets, while keeping each batch fast.
    BATCH_SIZE = 200
    for i in range(0, len(rows), BATCH_SIZE):
        batch = rows[i : i + BATCH_SIZE]
        cursor.executemany(insert_sql, batch)
        print(f"  -> inserted {min(i + BATCH_SIZE, len(rows))} / {len(rows)} chunks")

    connection.commit()
    print(f"  -> all {len(rows)} chunks inserted")

    print("\nStep 8: build vector index")
    safe_execute(cursor, "DROP INDEX DOC_CHUNKS_VEC_IDX")
    safe_execute(cursor, "DROP INDEX DOC_CHUNKS_HNSW_IDX")  # legacy name from earlier runs

    # Try HNSW first (in-memory, fast). If the database's VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE
    # isn't configured for HNSW (ORA-51962), fall back to IVF, which uses
    # regular tablespace storage and works without any memory config.
    # Both index types serve VECTOR_DISTANCE() queries the same way from the
    # application's perspective; the difference is internal storage.
    try:
        cursor.execute(
            """
            CREATE VECTOR INDEX DOC_CHUNKS_VEC_IDX
            ON DOC_CHUNKS (embedding)
            ORGANIZATION INMEMORY NEIGHBOR GRAPH
            DISTANCE COSINE
            WITH TARGET ACCURACY 95
            """
        )
        print("  -> HNSW vector index DOC_CHUNKS_VEC_IDX created (in-memory)")
    except oracledb.DatabaseError as exc:
        (error,) = exc.args
        if error.code == 51962:
            print("  -> VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE is not configured for HNSW")
            print("     Falling back to IVF index (uses regular tablespace storage)")
            print("     For HNSW in production, set VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE >= 512M in your DB")
            cursor.execute(
                """
                CREATE VECTOR INDEX DOC_CHUNKS_VEC_IDX
                ON DOC_CHUNKS (embedding)
                ORGANIZATION NEIGHBOR PARTITIONS
                DISTANCE COSINE
                WITH TARGET ACCURACY 95
                """
            )
            print("  -> IVF vector index DOC_CHUNKS_VEC_IDX created")
        else:
            raise

    cursor.close()
    connection.close()
    print(f"\nDone. Make sure your .env has ORACLE_USER={APP_USER}, then run:")
    print("  streamlit run app.py")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    try:
        main()
    except oracledb.DatabaseError as exc:
        print(f"\nDatabase error: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    except requests.exceptions.RequestException as exc:
        print(f"\nDownload error: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
